Message Subject: January 2026 Month-End Close Kickoff
Message Body:
Team,

Kicking off the January 2026 close. The close calendar, task assignments, and key milestones are attached. A few things to note this month:

- Final day for January activity is Friday 1/30. All sub-ledger postings need to be locked by COB on BD01 (2/2).
- We had two carry-over items from December: the APAC lease modification reclass and the EMEA intercompany timing difference on the consulting agreement. Both need to be resolved by BD02 at the latest.
- The board package is due to Amanda by BD10 this month, one day earlier than usual.

I will hold a 15-minute alignment call at 9:00 AM ET today with regional close leads. Thomas and Yuki, please confirm you can join.

Elena

Message Subject: RE: January 2026 Month-End Close Kickoff
Message Body:
Elena,

Confirmed for the 9 AM call. One flag from our side: the UK entity has a bank holiday on Monday 2/2, so our BD01 posting window is effectively one day shorter. Samuel and I have already shifted our EMEA sub-ledger timeline forward by half a day to compensate. I will walk through the adjusted schedule on the call.

Thomas

Message Subject: RE: RE: January 2026 Month-End Close Kickoff
Message Body:
Elena,

Apologies I could not join the call live due to the time difference. I reviewed the recording and the calendar. APAC is aligned on all milestones. The lease modification reclass from December is already drafted and will post on BD01 once Nguyen Thi Mai completes her review.

One item to flag: our India entity controller mentioned that the statutory auditors have requested certain schedules by February 10, which overlaps with our BD07 deliverables. I will manage the sequencing on our end but wanted you to be aware in case it affects the consolidation timeline.

Yuki

The Canada entity data is on track. Their controller sent the preliminary package this morning and I do not see any flags.

Message Subject: Revenue Contract Review - January Period Allocation
Message Body:
Robert,

I have completed the review of open revenue contracts for January period allocation. The population includes 34 active contracts with revenue recognition implications for this period.

Key items:
- TechWave Industries: Contract modification executed 1/15. The mod adds $1.2M in professional services over 6 months. Under ASC 606, this is a prospective treatment since the remaining performance obligations are distinct. January allocation is $200K.
- Apex Partners: Multi-element arrangement with hardware, software, and support. Stand-alone selling prices have been updated this quarter. Total contract value is $3.4M. January recognition is $340K for the hardware component (shipped 1/20) and $85K for the first month of the software subscription.
- Pinnacle Corp: Variable consideration adjustment. Quarterly rebate threshold was met in January, triggering a $165K catch-up that should be recognized this month.

All supporting schedules are attached.

Fatima

Message Subject: RE: Revenue Contract Review - January Period Allocation
Message Body:
Fatima,

Good work. The Pinnacle variable consideration catch-up needs to be reviewed by James Porter or his team before we book it. Variable consideration adjustments over $100K require technical accounting sign-off per our policy. Can you send the support to James today so we do not hold up the BD01 posting?

The TechWave and Apex items look straightforward. Proceed with the standard recognition entries for those.

Robert

Message Subject: Payroll Pre-Close Validation - January
Message Body:
Melissa,

US payroll pre-close validation is in progress. Tyler and I have been reconciling the SuccessFactors data against the payroll input files. So far:

- Headcount reconciliation: 1,247 active employees in SuccessFactors vs. 1,247 in the payroll system. Clean match.
- Overtime hours: we identified a $14K discrepancy between the time tracking system and the payroll file for the manufacturing group. This looks similar to the integration issue we saw in November where the system drops overtime entries that span a pay period boundary.
- Bonus approvals: 8 of 11 approved bonuses have been loaded. The remaining 3 are pending HR confirmation.

Tyler is working through the overtime issue now.

Steven

Message Subject: RE: Payroll Pre-Close Validation - January
Message Body:
Steven,

The overtime discrepancy - is this the same type of issue we saw in November? If so, I want to flag it to Kevin O'Donnell as a potential system integration issue between the time tracking platform and the payroll system. We should not be catching this manually every month.

Otherwise, the numbers look solid. I will wait for the EMEA and APAC confirmations before reporting final status to Elena.

Melissa

Message Subject: Missed Billing Cutoff - Orion Analytics Contract
Message Body:
David,

The Orion Analytics contract ($680K annual, first billing of $170K) missed the 3:00 PM cutoff. Legal finalized the contract approvals at 2:47 PM but the billing setup requires a new customer master record, pricing configuration, and tax setup, which cannot be completed in 13 minutes.

The services under this contract started on January 15, so there is a legitimate argument that revenue should be recognized in January even though the invoice will not be generated until February. I wanted to flag this for your direction on how to handle the revenue side.

Noah

Message Subject: RE: Missed Billing Cutoff - Orion Analytics Contract
Message Body:
Noah,

Defer the invoice to February. There is no getting around the operational cutoff.

However, the revenue recognition question is separate from the billing timing. Fatima, I need you to assess whether revenue can still be recognized in January based on the performance obligations satisfied during the month. If the service start date was 1/15 and services were delivered through month-end, we may have earned half a month's revenue regardless of the billing delay. Pull the contract terms and let me know by BD01.

David

Recipient Department(s): Accounting Operations|Accounts Payable (AP)|Accounts Receivable (AR)|Payroll & Benefits|Compliance|Tax|External Reporting|Finance
Message Subject: January 2026 Close Checklist - BD01 Readiness

All,

Attached is the updated close checklist reflecting pre-close status. All BD-3 through BD-1 tasks are complete or on track. A few items to be aware of heading into BD01:

- AP: Three late invoices ($240K) are being accrued under the rush exception. Distribution list issue has been corrected.

- Payroll: A $175K late bonus accrual will be booked as a manual entry outside the standard payroll journal.

- APAC: India account mapping bridge is in place for 15 accounts. Top-side adjustment may be needed at consolidation.

BD01 activities begin Monday 2/2. The late JE cutoff is 6:00 PM ET per the corrected calendar. Please have your teams ready to post by 8:00 AM.

Elena

Message Subject: CRITICAL - Duplicate Records in AP Interface Load
Message Body:
Robert, Elena,

The overnight AP interface batch loaded duplicate records for a subset of transactions. The batch file from the procurement system contained 214 invoice line items, but 38 of those were duplicated, inflating the AP sub-ledger by $347K.

I have confirmed the duplicates by matching document numbers. The root cause appears to be a retry logic error in the procurement system's outbound file generation. IT has been notified.

I need direction on next steps. If anyone has already started posting downstream entries against the inflated AP balance, we could have a cascading problem.

Kevin

Message Subject: RE: CRITICAL - Duplicate Records in AP Interface Load
Message Body:
Kevin,

Suspend all dependent AP processing immediately until this is cleaned up. Anthony, hold your team from posting final AP sub-ledger entries until Kevin confirms the reversal is complete.

Kevin, reverse the entire duplicate batch. Then have IT validate the procurement interface and rerun it with the corrected logic. I want confirmation that the rerun file matches the expected 214 line items exactly before you reload.

Preserve the original batch file and the corrected version as evidence. Jasmine Lee will need both for the SOX control documentation.

Elena

Message Subject: RE: RE: CRITICAL - Duplicate Records in AP Interface Load
Message Body:
Elena,

Duplicate batch has been reversed. IT corrected the retry logic and reran the interface. The corrected file contains exactly 214 line items matching the procurement system source. I have validated the totals and the reload is complete.

Anthony, your team is clear to resume AP processing. The AP sub-ledger balance is now correct.

I have saved both the original and corrected batch files in the close evidence folder. I will also prepare an RCA memo for Jasmine.

Kevin

Message Subject: Fixed Asset Depreciation Run - January 2026
Message Body:

Depreciation run is complete for all entities. Total depreciation expense for January: $1.28M.

No anomalies in the run. The fully depreciated Japan IT equipment disposal has been processed with zero gain/loss.

Emily

Intangible asset amortization entries are posted for January. Total amortization: $215K.

Message Subject: Revenue Engine Exception - ClearPath Systems Contract Amendment
Message Body:
Robert, James,

The revenue recognition engine generated an exception for the ClearPath Systems contract. They signed an amendment on January 8 that added implementation services to an existing software license. The engine treated the amendment as a prospective modification, but based on my review, the new services are not distinct from the original license. Under ASC 606-10-25-13, this should be a cumulative catch-up adjustment.

The prospective treatment recognized $45K in January. The cumulative catch-up treatment would recognize $112K (reflecting the reallocation of total consideration across all remaining performance obligations from contract inception). The difference is $67K.

James, I need a technical accounting determination on this. My analysis supports the cumulative catch-up, but the contract language is ambiguous on whether the implementation services can function independently.

Fatima

Message Subject: RE: Revenue Engine Exception - ClearPath Systems Contract Amendment
Message Body:
Fatima,

I reviewed the amendment and the original contract. You are correct that the cumulative catch-up treatment applies here. The implementation services require access to the proprietary software platform and cannot function independently, which means the added services are not distinct.

Override the engine output for ClearPath and book the manual true-up of $67K. Please prepare a technical accounting memo documenting the analysis under ASC 606-10-25-12 through 25-13. I will countersign it.

Also, Fatima, can you submit a rule change request to Kevin so the engine handles this pattern correctly in future periods? We should not be catching this manually for every similar amendment.

James

Message Subject: RE: RE: Revenue Engine Exception - ClearPath Systems Contract Amendment
Message Body:
Robert, James,

Manual true-up of $67K posted. Technical accounting memo is attached. I will send the rule change request to Kevin tomorrow.

On the Orion Analytics question from David Lawson: I have completed my assessment. The contract service start date was January 15, and performance obligations are satisfied over time. We can recognize half a month of revenue ($85K) in January even though the invoice will not be generated until February. I will post the revenue accrual and send David the support.

Fatima

Message Subject: SBC Forfeiture Assumptions - Version Verification

The file you received last week would not have included those.

The difference is probably small for three departures, but I want to make sure we are using the most current data.

Robert

Message Subject: RE: SBC Forfeiture Assumptions - Version Verification
Message Body:
Robert,

You are right. I used the file dated 1/23, not the revised one from 1/30. I just pulled the updated version and reran the calculation. The impact of the three additional departures is an $8K decrease in SBC expense. That is below our posting threshold, but since you flagged it, I will adjust and repost.

Updated total: $420K.

Christopher

Message Subject: FX Revaluation - January Month-End Rates Applied
Message Body:
Elena,

FX revaluation is complete for January. Kevin loaded the month-end rates from the treasury management system this morning and I ran the revaluation across all entities.

The revaluation entries are posted to the FX gain/loss account (6800-000). The $48K residual IC difference that Lauren flagged has washed through as expected.

Robert

Message Subject: RE: FX Revaluation - January Month-End Rates Applied
Message Body:
Robert,

Confirmed. The SAP rate table matches the treasury management system rates for all 8 currency pairs. No discrepancies.

Message Subject: GR/IR Review - Aged Unmatched Receipts Over $50K
Message Body:
Anthony, Heather,

During the GR/IR detail review, I found a population of aged unmatched receipts that are not part of the standard GRNI accrual. These are goods receipt entries from November and December where we received the goods but have never received an invoice.

Total population: $215K across 9 POs. Three of the POs ($145K combined) are for IT hardware that was received in November. The vendor (TechSupply Global) is not responding to our outreach. The remaining 6 POs ($70K) are for various services where invoices are simply late.

Should we accrue the full $215K, or apply a threshold?

Sophia

Message Subject: RE: GR/IR Review - Aged Unmatched Receipts Over $50K
Message Body:
Sophia,

Accrue the full $215K. We have goods receipts confirming delivery, which gives us sufficient evidence that we owe the money regardless of invoice timing.

For TechSupply Global specifically, the $145K is significant and the lack of invoice for two months is unusual. Anthony, please have procurement reach out through their vendor management contact. If we still have not received an invoice by next close, we may need to re-examine whether the PO terms are enforceable.

For the other 6 POs, accrue at receipt value and leave them in the aged population for monitoring.

Heather

Message Subject: General Operating Accruals - January Posted
Message Body:
Robert,

General operating accruals for January are posted. Total: $680K.

Major items:
- Professional services accrual (legal, audit, consulting): $285K
- Facilities and maintenance accrual: $142K
- Marketing program accruals: $95K
- IT services and licensing accruals: $88K
- Other operating accruals: $70K

The professional services accrual includes $125K for the external audit fee estimate. I used the engagement letter amount prorated for the month, consistent with our methodology.

All accruals are supported by underlying contracts, purchase orders, or budget owner confirmations.

Christopher

Message Subject: AP Recon Finding - Direct GL Posting to Control Account
Message Body:
Anthony, Robert,

During the AP reconciliation, I found a $38K entry posted directly to the AP control account in the GL that did not originate from the AP sub-ledger. The entry was posted by a member of the general accounting team as a manual accrual for a vendor settlement.

The economics appear to be correct (it is a legitimate vendor obligation), but posting directly to the control account bypasses the sub-ledger and makes the reconciliation unreliable. If I had not caught it, the AP sub-ledger would have been $38K short of the GL.

How do you want to handle this?

Sophia

Message Subject: RE: AP Recon Finding - Direct GL Posting to Control Account
Message Body:
Sophia,

Reverse the direct GL posting. Then have the general accounting team rebook the vendor settlement through the AP sub-ledger using the proper invoice or accrual workflow. If the sub-ledger cannot accommodate the entry type (which it should for an accrual), then record it as a clearly documented reconciling item with my approval.

The control account should only be touched through the sub-ledger. I am going to reinforce this with the team.

Robert

Message Subject: Segment Reporting Data - January 2026
Message Body:
Robert, Priya,

Segment reporting data for January is prepared:

- Americas segment: Revenue $26.2M, Operating income $5.8M (margin 22.1%)
- EMEA segment: Revenue $8.1M, Operating income $1.2M (margin 14.8%)
- APAC segment: Revenue $4.2M, Operating income $0.3M (margin 7.1%)

APAC margin is lower than usual due to the India restructuring charge ($195K) being allocated to that segment. Excluding the charge, APAC margin would be 11.7%, which is in line with plan.

The segment allocations are consistent with our allocation methodology and tie to the consolidated results.

Fatima

Message Subject: Non-GAAP Reconciliation - January Adjustments
Message Body:
Hannah, James,

Draft non-GAAP reconciliation for January:

GAAP operating income: $7.30M

Adjustments:
- SBC expense: +$420K
- Amortization of acquired intangibles: +$215K
- India restructuring charge: +$195K

Non-GAAP operating income: $8.13M (21.1% non-GAAP operating margin)

I have not included the QuantumEdge bad debt reserve as a non-GAAP adjustment. While it is specific to one customer, bad debt expense is a recurring operating cost and excluding it would not meet our policy criteria for non-GAAP treatment.

The restructuring charge is the one I want to confirm. Hannah's question from BD06 about whether it qualifies: my view is yes, it meets our policy definition of a non-recurring charge related to a discrete business reorganization.

Mia

Message Subject: RE: Non-GAAP Reconciliation - January Adjustments
Message Body:
Mia,

I agree on both points. The restructuring charge qualifies as a non-GAAP adjustment under our policy. The QuantumEdge reserve does not, and I am glad you did not include it. Excluding individual bad debt charges is a slippery slope.

Proceed with the reconciliation as drafted.

James

Message Subject: January Close Retrospective Preparation
Message Body:
Robert, Priya,

I am preparing the January close retrospective for the BD12 meeting. I need both of you to send me your input by end of day:

Robert: Close execution summary. What went well, what did not, specific process breakdowns, and recommended fixes. I know the SAP issues and the AP interface incident are top of mind, but I also want your view on the overall posting workflow efficiency.

Priya: Close metrics comparison vs. the prior three months. Trend analysis on cycle time, adjustment volume, and control exceptions. I want to see whether the January exception count (4) is an outlier or part of a pattern.

The retrospective meeting is scheduled for tomorrow at 10:00 AM ET.

Elena

Message Subject: RE: January Close Retrospective Preparation
Message Body:
Elena,

Here is my input:

What went well:
- All posting deadlines met despite the BD01 interface disruption
- IC reconciliation was the cleanest in 6 months (Lauren's process improvements are paying off)
- Regional coordination was strong, particularly APAC (India complexity was managed effectively)
- Revenue recognition engine exceptions were resolved same-day with technical accounting support

What needs improvement:
- SAP configuration management. Two incidents (period config, approval routing) both trace to inadequate change management. Kevin has remediation plans for both.
- AP interface reliability. The duplicate batch issue cost us 2 hours on BD01.
- Benefits rate update process. The fact that we entered the year with stale rates is a planning failure.
- New entity onboarding. BangaTech LLP should have been on the tax schedule before the close started.

Recommendations:
- Add a January-specific pre-close checklist for year-start items (benefit rates, tax rate updates, new entity verification)
- Implement duplicate detection logic in the AP interface (Kevin and IT are working on this)
- Require SAP configuration changes to go through a formal change ticket, even for routine period operations

Robert

Message Subject: Credit Memo Requests - Cutoff Concern
Message Body:
David, Fatima,

Four credit memo requests came in this morning totaling $145K:

1. MeridianTech: $62K for a feature downgrade. Approval from sales director, support documentation complete.
2. CloudBridge: $38K for a service level penalty. Approval from ops VP, SLA report attached.
3. NexGen Corp: $28K for contract price adjustment. Approval from sales manager only (below the $50K threshold that requires director sign-off).
4. Horizon Partners: $17K for a duplicate billing correction. Documentation looks thin. No formal approval, just an email from the account manager.

Items 1-3 have sufficient support. Item 4 concerns me. There is no evidence of the duplicate billing in the system, and the account manager's email does not explain the basis.

David, how do you want me to handle Item 4?

Noah

Message Subject: RE: Credit Memo Requests - Cutoff Concern
Message Body:
Noah,

Process items 1-3 in January. They have proper approvals and documentation.

Hold item 4 (Horizon Partners $17K). Do not process it until we have evidence of the duplicate billing and a proper approval. If the account manager cannot substantiate the claim by BD02, defer it to February and let the AR team investigate.

We are not issuing credit memos based on unsupported requests, regardless of the amount.

David

Message Subject: Revenue Deferral Error - Billing System Date Mapping
Message Body:
Robert, James,

During my deferred revenue reconciliation, I discovered that the billing system is pulling the wrong service start date for a subset of contracts. The system is using the contract execution date rather than the service commencement date for the deferral schedule.

For 8 contracts, the service commencement date is 15 to 30 days after the execution date. The result is that revenue is being recognized earlier than it should be. The cumulative overstatement in January is approximately $54K.

I can post a manual deferral correction to reverse the $54K and establish the correct recognition schedule going forward. But the source system mapping needs to be fixed to prevent this from recurring.

Robert

Message Subject: RE: Revenue Deferral Error - Billing System Date Mapping
Message Body:
Fatima,

Book the $54K manual deferral correction in January. Revenue should be recognized based on the actual service delivery period, not the contract execution date.

Kevin, add the billing system date mapping fix to the IT remediation queue. Fatima, also add a reasonableness test to your monthly revenue review that compares recognized revenue per contract to the expected recognition based on contract term profiles. That would have caught this pattern earlier.

James, heads up that this is a similar issue to the ClearPath engine exception, just in the other direction. The billing system and the revenue engine are not always aligned on dates.

Robert

Message Subject: Cash Flow Statement Mapping Error - Contract Asset Account
Message Body:
Priya, Kevin,

The draft cash flow statement does not tie to the balance sheet. The $85K contract asset for Orion Analytics was booked to a new GL account (1425-000) that is not in the cash flow mapping template. The system treated it as an unmapped item and excluded it from the cash flow statement entirely.

The cash flow statement shows total assets decreasing by $85K less than the balance sheet indicates, which breaks the tie-out.

I need to add this account to the cash flow mapping. The correct classification is "Operating Activities - Changes in operating assets" (increase in contract assets). The fix is straightforward but it means the statements I generated this morning need to be regenerated.

Daniel

Message Subject: RE: Cash Flow Statement Mapping Error - Contract Asset Account
Message Body:
Daniel,

Correct the mapping and regenerate the statements. The cash flow needs to tie before we distribute the management package.

Kevin, can we add a control so that any time a new GL account is created, the cash flow mapping is reviewed and updated before the next close? This is the kind of thing that should be caught at account setup, not during the reporting phase.

Priya

Message Subject: FX Rate Table - SGD/USD Rate Discrepancy
Message Body:
Kevin,

I noticed a small discrepancy in the FX revaluation results. The SGD/USD rate in SAP is 1.3425, but the treasury management system shows 1.3412. The difference is producing about $1.8K of noise in the Singapore entity translation.

Can you check whether the correct rate table was loaded for the SGD pair? The rest of the currencies match.

Robert

Message Subject: RE: FX Rate Table - SGD/USD Rate Discrepancy
Message Body:
Robert,

You are right. The SGD rate was pulled from the mid-market rate feed instead of the closing bid rate that we use for the other pairs. I have corrected it to 1.3412 and rerun the Singapore revaluation. The $1.8K adjustment is posted.

All other pairs are confirmed matching.

Kevin

Message Subject: Compliance Post-Close Review - January

Mark, Jasmine,

I have reviewed the January SOX documentation package and Mark's deficiency assessment. A few directives:

Natalie

Message Subject: Payroll Time Tracking Integration - Recurring Issue
Message Body:
Kevin,

The overtime discrepancy Steven flagged in the payroll pre-close validation is confirmed as the same type of system integration issue we saw in November. The time tracking system drops overtime entries that span a pay period boundary when the data crosses the interface to the payroll system.

This is the second time in three months. Steven's team caught it both times through manual reconciliation, but we cannot rely on manual detection as a permanent control.

Can you prioritize this with IT? I would like a fix in place before the February close if possible. The January impact was $14K, but the risk of a larger miss is real if the manual review ever slips.

Melissa

Message Subject: RE: Payroll Time Tracking Integration - Recurring Issue
Message Body:
Melissa,

Understood. I will escalate to IT today. The issue is likely in the boundary-handling logic of the interface. I will request a targeted fix and have IT include it in the interface remediation work they are already doing for the AP duplicate batch issue.

Target for fix: March close. I cannot guarantee February given the scope of the interface work already in progress.

Kevin

Message Subject: ASC 842 - Singapore Lease Modification Details
Message Body:
Robert, James,

Details on the Singapore office expansion lease modification that was carried over from December:

- Original lease: 5,000 sq ft, SGD 12K/month, 4 years remaining
- Modification: Added 2,000 sq ft at SGD 5.2K/month for the remaining term
- Modification date: December 15, 2025
- Classification: Operating lease (no change from original)

I treated this as a separate lease component since the additional space is priced at a rate consistent with the standalone market rent. Under ASC 842-10-25-8, this qualifies as a separate contract.

The new ROU asset for the expansion: SGD 230K (present value of the additional payments at 5.2% IBR). Amortization starts January 1. January amortization: SGD 4.8K.

James, does my treatment align with your read of the modification guidance? The key question is whether the additional space is distinct.

Mei Lin

Message Subject: RE: ASC 842 - Singapore Lease Modification Details
Message Body:
Mei Lin,

Your treatment is correct. The additional 2,000 sq ft is a distinct increment (it can be used independently of the original space) and the pricing is consistent with market rates for comparable space in the same building. This meets the criteria under ASC 842-10-25-8 for a separate contract.

The 5.2% IBR is reasonable given the Singapore entity's credit profile and current market conditions. Proceed with the entry.

James

Message Subject: Prepaids Recon - Contract Asset Classification Question
Message Body:
Robert, Fatima,

During the prepaids reconciliation, I noticed that the $85K Orion Analytics contract asset is sitting in the "Other Current Assets" line within prepaids. Should this be reclassified to a separate contract asset line on the balance sheet?

The amount is relatively small, but with the Apex Partners deal and potentially more contracts being recognized ahead of billing, the contract asset population could grow. If we are going to break it out, it is easier to do it now than to reclassify later when the balance is larger.

Message Subject: SBC Treatment Question - Continued Vesting on Negotiated Termination
Message Body:
Andrew, Hannah,

Looping you in on an SBC accounting question that came out of the Q1 equity recon. Senior Director with negotiated continued vesting through 6/30/2026 (separation agreement signed Q4). Shareworks defaulted to forfeiture; we are overriding to recognize SBC through the modified vesting period.

Question: under ASC 718, do you treat this as a Type III modification (improbable to probable) or do we just continue original-grant expense recognition through the new vesting end? My read is the latter since vesting was extended without changing fair value or probability, but I want to confirm before the SBC number lands in the close package.

No financial statement impact difference for Q1, but the disclosure language for Q2 onward will differ.

Rachel

Message Subject: RE: SBC Treatment Question - Continued Vesting on Negotiated Termination
Message Body:
Rachel,

Agreed with your read. This is a service-condition modification under ASC 718, but since the fair value at modification date and the probability of vesting both remain consistent with the original terms (extended vesting being a known and committed obligation), it is a Type I scenario - continue recognizing the original grant-date fair value over the new requisite service period.

No modification charge required. Just continue expensing the unvested portion through 6/30/2026.

For disclosure: we will need a footnote reference in the 10-Q describing the modification. Hannah and I will draft language. Please send us the final SBC figures once posted.

Andrew

Message Subject: RE: RE: SBC Treatment Question - Continued Vesting on Negotiated Termination
Message Body:
Andrew,

Thanks for the quick turnaround. I will finalize the calculation today and send the SBC figures over tomorrow once the recon is posted. I will reference your treatment guidance in the working paper.

Rachel

Message Subject: Decision Needed - FX Reval Timing vs CIO Accrual Rework
Message Body:
John, Elena,

Need a quick decision. The CIO accrual JE was rejected this morning for retired cost centers (Mei caught it at 8:55 AM). Nia has resubmitted the corrected template, but we are now sitting at 3:40 PM on BD+4 and our scheduled FX revaluation run is supposed to start at 4:00 PM.

Two options:

1. Hold the FX reval until the CIO accrual posts. Realistic timing: CIO accrual posts by 5:30 PM, FX reval starts around 6:00 PM, wraps overnight. This pushes my BD+5 sequence by roughly half a day and tightens the GL recon review window.

2. Run FX reval at 4:00 PM as scheduled and post the CIO accrual into the open period as a same-day correction afterward. Reval finishes on time, but the CIO accrual amounts will not be reflected in the FX-revalued balances until the manual correction posts. The audit trail will show a post-reval correction, which I want to flag before doing it.

Amount on the CIO accrual is $187K, all USD-denominated, so the FX impact of the timing difference itself is zero. The concern is purely the audit trail and the fact that the standard close sequence shows CIO accrual posting before FX reval.

My recommendation is option 2 - run reval on schedule, post the correction with a clear documentation trail. But I want your call on this given the audit-trail visibility.

Robert

Message Subject: RE: Decision Needed - FX Reval Timing vs CIO Accrual Rework
Message Body:
Robert,

Go with option 2. Run the FX reval on schedule. Post the CIO correction afterward with a memo on the JE noting (a) the original rejection on 2/4 morning, (b) the resubmission timing, and (c) the rationale for posting after FX reval given the entry is USD-denominated and creates no FX impact.

Loop Mark Feldman in on the SOX evidence side - he should know we deviated from the standard sequence and have the documentation ready in case audit asks.

Do not let this become a habit. If we see another JE rejection cause cascade pressure on FX reval next quarter, we need to revisit the BD+4 packing.

John

Message Subject: RE: RE: Decision Needed - FX Reval Timing vs CIO Accrual Rework
Message Body:
John,

Understood. Adding Mark on this thread for SOX awareness. FX reval kicks off at 4:00 PM as scheduled. CIO accrual will post tonight once Mei completes validation, with a memo on the JE per your guidance. Documentation will be filed in the close working papers folder under Q1_BD4_Sequence_Exception.

Elena, please flag in tomorrow's stand-up that we want to review BD+4 packing for next quarter.

Robert

Message Subject: Late Contracts from Legal - Rev Rec Engine Timing at Risk
Message Body:
Rebecca, Gregory, John,

Legal forwarded a batch of late-signed contracts at 3:15 PM today. Two are large enterprise deals - Northstar Industries ($4.2M ACV) and Calderon Group ($3.8M ACV) - both with non-standard ramp clauses I have not seen before:

- Northstar: phased go-live across 4 quarters with revenue recognition triggers tied to specific milestone acceptances (signed by their CIO).
- Calderon: deferred go-live (May 1) with retroactive billing back to contract signing date once go-live confirmed.

Both need contract modeling in NetSuite before the rev rec engine can run cleanly. My team estimates 1.5 to 2 days of work to get the modeling correct - including a discussion with Legal on the Calderon clause language. That puts us at risk of missing the BD+4 rev rec engine slot, and would cascade into the rest of the close.

Option I want to propose: run the rev rec engine on schedule using a documented estimate for these two contracts based on our current best read of the terms. True up next quarter once modeling is complete and Legal has signed off. Combined Q1 revenue impact of the estimate vs final modeling is unlikely to exceed $400K based on a quick read - well below our quarterly materiality threshold.

Need your call on this by tomorrow morning so I can plan the BD+1 NetSuite cleanup queue accordingly.

Marcus

Message Subject: RE: Late Contracts from Legal - Rev Rec Engine Timing at Risk
Message Body:
Marcus,

I agree with the estimate-and-true-up approach in principle, but I want to push back on the materiality framing. $400K is below quarterly materiality, but if both contracts end up on the high side of our estimate range, we could be closer to $700K combined. That is still below threshold but it is not a comfortable margin.

Proposing two refinements:

1. Use a deliberately conservative estimate (lower bound of revenue recognition for both deals) so any true-up next quarter is positive rather than negative. A negative true-up would be much harder to explain to the audit committee.

2. Get Legal sign-off on our reading of the Calderon retroactive billing clause before we lock the estimate. If they read it differently than we do, the estimate range changes.

If John concurs, I would proceed on that basis.

Rebecca

Message Subject: RE: RE: Late Contracts from Legal - Rev Rec Engine Timing at Risk
Message Body:
Marcus, Rebecca,

Approved on the estimate-and-true-up approach with Rebecca's two conditions. Specifically:

1. Use the conservative (lower bound) revenue estimate for both contracts. Document the lower-bound assumption explicitly so the true-up direction is known up front.

2. Get a written read from Legal on the Calderon retroactive billing clause before the estimate is finalized. If their interpretation diverges from ours, we revisit before the rev rec run.

Decision memo for the working papers should cover: contracts flagged, materiality assessment, estimate methodology, sign-offs, and the commitment to true up in Q2. I want to see the memo before Marcus runs the engine.

For reporting: Andrew Li and Hannah Sinclair need to know about this for the 10-Q estimate disclosure language. Loop them in once the memo is drafted.

John

Message Subject: RE: RE: RE: Late Contracts from Legal - Rev Rec Engine Timing at Risk
Message Body:
John, Rebecca,

Understood. Plan of record:

- Today (BD+1): I draft the decision memo, send Legal the Calderon clause for written interpretation, build the conservative-bound estimate models for both contracts.
- Tomorrow (BD+2): Confirm Legal interpretation, finalize estimates, circulate memo for sign-off, flag the two contracts as known exceptions in the NetSuite error queue (so they do not block the rev rec run).
- BD+4: Run the rev rec engine on schedule with the estimates baked in.
- Q2: True up once both contracts are properly modeled.

Andrew and Hannah will be looped in once the memo is in draft.

Marcus
